brent.label: Brent Crude $/bbl
brent.change: +33.5% since Feb 27 — Brent $94.79/bbl (Apr 14 close) — DOWN 4.2% on peace-talk optimism — Apr 15 intraday $95-96 — Bloomberg: 2-WEEK CEASEFIRE EXTENSION under consideration
brent.tooltip: Brent Crude: The international benchmark. Pre-war close: $71.00/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 14 close: $94.79/bbl (-$4.14, -4.2% — settling dramatically lower on peace-talk optimism after the White House signaled willingness to resume Islamabad talks). Apr 15 intraday mixed: ~$95.00-96.30, slightly higher (+0.2 to +1.6%). VP Vance: 'the ball is in Iran's court.' API reported US crude inventories rose 6.1M barrels — the 8th consecutive weekly build. IEA warned this conflict could trigger the first annual oil demand decline since the 2020 pandemic. OPEC+ output fell 7.9 mb/d in March due to the conflict. Bloomberg 15:12 UTC Apr 15: US and Iran reportedly considering EXTENDING THE CEASEFIRE BY TWO WEEKS. Pakistan Army Chief Asim Munir arrived in Tehran Apr 15 carrying 'new message from Washington.' Round 2 talks 'could be happening over next two days' (Trump to NY Post). IRGC Maj. Gen. Abdollahi Apr 15 threatens counter-blockade of Persian Gulf + Sea of Oman + Red Sea — significant rhetorical escalation but no kinetic action. Source: ICE Futures Europe / Bloomberg / API / IEA / CNN / Trump.

wti.label: WTI Crude $/bbl
wti.change: +37.3% since Feb 27 — WTI $91.28/bbl (Apr 14 close) — DOWN 7.7% (-$7.59) on peace-talk optimism — Apr 15 intraday $90.90-93.73 — gas $4.108
wti.tooltip: West Texas Intermediate: The US benchmark. Pre-war close: $66.50/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 14 close: $91.28/bbl (-$7.59, -7.7% — largest single-day drop since the Apr 8 ceasefire rally). Apr 15 intraday mixed $90.90-93.73 (Trading Economics $90.92 -0.40%; Investing.com $93.73 — ~$2.80 spread reflects futures vs CFD vs spot timestamps). AAA national average gas: $4.108/gal Apr 15 (down 1 cent from Apr 14's $4.118). California leads at $5.878; Kansas cheapest at $3.507. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent Apr 15: 'optimistic that sometime between June 20 and September 20, we can have $3 gas again.' API reports US crude inventories rose 6.1M barrels — 8th consecutive weekly build. Blockade Day 3 (48-hr CENTCOM update): 9 vessels turned back, no breaches; 20+ non-Iran-bound vessels passed freely. March CPI 3.3% YoY / +0.9% MoM — gasoline +21.2% MoM largest since 1967. Markets now price almost no rate cut at Apr 28-29 FOMC. Source: NYMEX / AAA / BLS / API / Bessent / CENTCOM.

gas.label: US Gas Avg/Gal
gas.change: +$1.128 / +37.9% since Feb 27 — $4.108 nationally (AAA Apr 15) — DOWN 1 cent from Apr 14 — California $5.878 — Bessent: 'optimistic' about $3 gas by June-September
gas.tooltip: National average retail gasoline price. Pre-war: $2.98/gal (Feb 27). Current: $4.108/gal nationally (AAA Apr 15) — down 1 cent from Apr 14's $4.118 on continuing peace-talk optimism. Most expensive state: California at $5.878; cheapest: Kansas at $3.507. Gas crossed $4.00/gallon Apr 2 for first time since Aug 2022. Diesel national average ~$5.43/gallon (+50.2% YoY per SmartAsset). Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent Apr 15: 'optimistic that sometime between June 20 and September 20, we can have $3 gas again.' March CPI (released Apr 10) confirmed gasoline prices surged 21.2% in a single month — the largest monthly spike since CPI was first published in 1967. Headline CPI 3.3% YoY (up from 2.4% Feb). Core CPI tame at +0.2% MoM / +2.6% YoY. Consumer cumulative fuel cost through Apr 1: $14.5B (AEI). Iranian Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf posted Washington gas station prices, taunting: 'Enjoy the current pump figures... Soon you'll be nostalgic for $4-$5 gas.' Source: AAA / BLS / Bessent / SmartAsset / AEI.

sp500.label: S&P 500
sp500.change: +88 pts / +1.3% from pre-war — S&P 6,967.38 (Apr 14 close +1.18%) — Apr 15 NEW INTRADAY ATH 7,003.71 surpasses Jan 28 record — Nasdaq 10th straight up day — VIX 17.99
sp500.tooltip: S&P 500 index. Pre-war close: 6,878.88 (Feb 27). Apr 14 close: 6,967.38 (+81.14, +1.18%) — erasing all losses since the war began. Dow 48,535.99 (+317.74, +0.66%); Nasdaq Composite 23,639.08 (+1.96%); Russell 2000 2,705.67 (+1.32%); VIX 17.99 (-2.02%). Apr 15 NEW INTRADAY ATH 7,003.71 — first time above 7,000, surpassing Jan 28 record of 7,002.28 — on track for first record close since January. Nasdaq +0.9-1.0% to ~23,852-23,875 — 10th consecutive positive session. Dow slightly lower (-0.1 to -0.2%, -48-97 pts). Russell 2000 flat to +0.33%. VIX 17.74-18.36 (-3-5%). 10Y Treasury 4.248% Apr 14, ~4.274% Apr 15. Gold $4,830.31 Apr 15 (-0.24%) — range $4,800-4,847; rose 2% Apr 14 to highest since Mar 18. DXY 97.85 (6-week low). Bitcoin $74,068-74,828 (8-day rally testing $74,434-$76,159 resistance). Morgan Stanley's Mike Wilson: 'the lows are in.' Fundstrat's Tom Lee: markets pricing favorable Iran resolution. Bank of America Q1 EPS $1.11, Rev $30.3B (+7% YoY), stock +2.5%. Morgan Stanley Q1 RECORD EPS $3.43, RECORD Rev $20.58B, net income +29% YoY, ROTCE 27.1%, stock +5%+. ASML beat Q1, raised FY guidance $42.4-47.2B on AI demand. UMich consumer sentiment hit record low in April. Markets price ~no rate cut at Apr 28-29 FOMC. Source: NYSE / CNBC / Bloomberg / BofA / Morgan Stanley / ASML / UMich / Federal Reserve.

daily_cost.label: Est. Daily US Cost
daily_cost.note: ~$800M/day — BLOCKADE DAY 3 — 27 Navy vessels in CENTCOM (~41% of deployed fleet) — 16,500+ sailors/Marines — highest concentration since 2003 Iraq invasion
daily_cost.tooltip: Estimated daily operational cost. Day 47: ~$800M/day — burn rate moderated as active strike operations paused during ceasefire (now ~192 hours). Harvard Prof. Linda Bilmes (CNBC Apr 14): ~$2B/day active fighting — 'I am certain we will reach $1 trillion' including long-term costs. Penn Wharton Budget Model: $40-95B direct cost, up to $210B total economic impact. THAAD interceptors cost $12.7M each versus Iran's Shahed drones at $35,000 each — 363:1 ratio. More Patriot missiles fired in first 4 days of Iran war than US gave Ukraine in four years. CENTCOM theater: 27 Navy vessels (~41% of all deployed/underway ships globally); 16,500+ sailors and Marines — HIGHEST CONCENTRATION SINCE THE 2003 IRAQ INVASION. Three carrier strike groups: Lincoln (Arabian Sea — active combat ops / blockade enforcement, CVW-9 incl. F-35C, DESRON 21); Ford (Eastern Med — RECORD-BREAKING 9+ month deployment, CVW-8, DESRON 2); Bush (en route southwestern Africa, relieving Ford, CVW-7, DESRON 22). 6 independent destroyers in Arabian Sea (McFaul, John Finn, Milius, Delbert D. Black, Pinckney, Mitscher). 3 LCS in Persian Gulf (Canberra, Tulsa, Santa Barbara). USS Chief + USS Pioneer MCM departed Singapore Apr 11. Tripoli ARG + Boxer ARG deployed. 4 DDGs Eastern Med (Roosevelt, Bulkeley, Oscar Austin, Thomas Hudner). Ford fire incident (mid-March laundry compartment) took 30 hrs to extinguish, ~600 sailors lost bunks. USNI Apr 13: 291 total Battle Force ships (232 USS, 59 USNS), 105 deployed, 86 underway. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / AEI / Harvard Bilmes / Penn Wharton / CENTCOM / USNI Fleet Tracker.

total_cost.label: Est. Total Cost (47 Days)
total_cost.note: ~$53.35B through Day 47 — Manifest range: $52-55B (Day 47 consensus) — Harvard Bilmes: $1T long-term certain — Penn Wharton: up to $210B total economic impact — $200B supplemental pending
total_cost.tooltip: Cumulative estimated cost since Feb 28. Internal site tally: ~$53.35B (Day 47). External estimates Day 47: Iran-cost-ticker.com ~$52-53B (based on Pentagon $11.3B/6 days + $1B/day ongoing); AEI (Day 33): ~$50B total economic impact including govt costs + consumer fuel costs; Penn Wharton Budget Model: $40-95B direct, up to $210B total economic impact; Harvard Prof. Linda Bilmes (CNBC Apr 14): ~$2B/day active fighting, 'I am certain we will reach $1 trillion' including long-term/lifecycle costs. Accounting for reduced burn rate during ceasefire (less munitions expenditure offset by blockade costs), Day 47 estimate of $52-55B in direct military costs is reasonable. Pentagon has requested a $200B supplemental from Congress (quadrupled from initial $50B, not yet formally submitted); 71% of Americans oppose the spending. Rep. Boebert: 'a no on any war supplemental.' Cost to Iran (Iran govt estimate): ~$270B in damages. FDD: blockade costs Iran ~$435M/day. IMF Apr 14 cut 2026 global growth forecast to 3.1% (from 3.4%); Iran projected to contract 6.1%, Qatar -8.6%, Iraq -6.8%. Pew Research (Mar 16-22): 61% disapprove Iran handling; only 37% approve. 29% say war worth toll (vs 59% Iraq 2003). 60-day War Powers deadline Apr 29-May 1 — 14-16 days. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / AEI / Harvard Bilmes / Penn Wharton / WarCosts.org / FDD / IMF / Pew.

targets_struck.label: Targets Struck
targets_struck.note: CEASEFIRE ON IRAN FRONT HOLDS ~192 HRS — 35,500+ cumulative — no new strikes on Iranian territory Apr 13-15 — Battle of Bint Jbeil near-complete per IDF: only 'several dozen' Hezbollah remain — 100+ killed
targets_struck.tooltip: Total targets struck by US and Israeli forces since Feb 28. CENTCOM Adm. Brad Cooper: 13,000+ strikes destroyed 'the military Iran built for over 40 years' — 155+ warships/submarines (largest navy elimination since WWII), 80% air defenses, 450 ballistic missile storage, 800 drone storage, 90% weapons factories, every submarine, 97% naval mines. Cumulative: 900+ strikes in first 12 hours (Feb 28); 1,879+ US-Israeli strikes documented by ACLED through mid-March across 29 of 31 provinces. No confirmed US/Israeli strikes on Iranian territory since 8 PM ET Apr 7 (~192 hours, ceasefire technically holds). CNN Apr 14 satellite imagery shows Iran ACTIVELY RECONSTITUTING missile capabilities during the ceasefire — equipment, dump trucks, front-end loaders visible at blocked tunnel entrances near underground missile bases at Khomeyn and south of Tabriz; US intel assesses ~50% of Iran's missile launchers survived the first 40 days. CNN Apr 11: China preparing new air defense deliveries to Iran within weeks. On Lebanon front: Battle of Bint Jbeil NEAR-COMPLETE per IDF — only 'several dozen' Hezbollah fighters remain; 100+ killed since Apr 9 launch; IDF raided hospital where armed Hezbollah were sheltering; Apr 14: 10 IDF paratroopers wounded (3 severely), 1 KIA + 13 total WIA, 3 Radwan captured; Apr 15: IDF 8th Brigade uncovered anti-tank launchers + weapons caches, struck Hezbollah command center. 5 IDF divisions operating (36th, 91st, 98th, 146th, 162nd). Operation 'Silver Plough' demolishing all border village structures. Israel maintains Lebanon never part of ceasefire (VP Vance: 'we never made that promise'). Source: CENTCOM / IDF / Pentagon / USNI / CNN / WSJ / ACLED / Haaretz.

us_kia.label: US Military Deaths
us_kia.note: 13 hostile KIA + 1 non-combat death — 381 WIA (CENTCOM) — Intercept disputes: 15+ KIA, 520+ injuries — no new casualties since Apr 7 ceasefire (~192 hrs) — blockade Day 3 undirectly tested
us_kia.tooltip: US military deaths. CENTCOM official: 13 killed in hostile action plus 1 non-combat death (Maj. Sorffly Davius, Kuwait), 381 wounded (unchanged since Apr 8). Breakdown: 7 by direct enemy fire, 6 in KC-46/KC-135 crash in Iraq Mar 13. The Intercept disputes: at least 15 KIA and 520+ total injuries including USS Ford fire casualties (~200 sailors), F-15 shoot-down injuries, and A-10 crash near Hormuz excluded from official counts; DCAS internal data reportedly exceeds CENTCOM's public figures by 23%. Named KIA: Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington, SFC Noah Tietjens, Capt. Cody Khork. F-15E crew (494th Fighter Squadron, callsign 'Dude 44B') fully rescued Apr 4-5 from Zagros Mountains after 36-hour evasion. No new US casualties since ceasefire (8 PM ET Apr 7, ~192 hours). Combat losses include 1 F-15 (Apr 3, crew rescued), 1 A-10, multiple C-130s destroyed on ground; $1.4B+ combat losses/infrastructure damage in first 6 days (CSIS). Blockade Day 3 (Apr 15, 48-hr CENTCOM update): 9 vessels turned back, no breaches; 10,000+ personnel + 12+ warships deployed. IRGC warning: any military vessel approaching Strait triggers 'harsh and decisive' force — risk remains elevated. Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / Military Times / The Intercept / CSIS.

us_wia.label: US Service Members WIA
us_wia.note: 381 WIA (CENTCOM) — Intercept disputes: 520+ total injuries including USS Ford fire sailors (~200), F-15 shoot-down, A-10 crash — DCAS internal data reportedly 23% above CENTCOM — ceasefire ~192 hrs
us_wia.tooltip: US service members wounded/injured across all theaters since Feb 28. CENTCOM official: 381 wounded (unchanged since Apr 8) — 330 returned to duty, 5 seriously wounded. The Intercept disputes: at least 520+ total injuries including 200+ USS Ford fire sailors, F-15 shoot-down injuries, and A-10 crash near Hormuz excluded from official counts; DCAS internal data reportedly exceeds CENTCOM's public figures by 23%. Most injuries are traumatic brain injuries from blast overpressure. Ceasefire in effect from 8 PM ET Apr 7 (~192 hours). No confirmed new WIA since ceasefire. Blockade Day 3 (Apr 15, 48-hr CENTCOM update): 9 vessels turned back, no breaches; 10,000+ personnel deployed. IRGC warning: any military vessel approaching Strait triggers 'harsh and decisive' force — risk remains elevated. Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / Military Times / The Intercept.

iranian_killed.label: Iranian Killed (est.)
iranian_killed.note: Health Min: 2,076+ / HRANA: 3,636 (1,701 civilians, 1,221 military, 212+ children) / Iran Intl: 4,700+ security / Hengaw: 6,620 military (Apr 8) / Red Crescent: ~2,000 killed / 27,000 injuries — ceasefire ~192 hrs
iranian_killed.tooltip: Iranian casualties — deeply contested across sources with a nearly 4x spread. Iran Health Ministry (official): 2,076+ killed (Mar 31); Iran Red Crescent (Apr 10): ~2,000 fatalities, ~27,000 injuries. HRANA (US-based NGO, Apr 7): 3,636 total (1,701 civilians, 1,221 military, 714 unclassified, 212+ children per Iran MoH, 15% of total per HRANA). Iran International (Mar 31): 4,700+ security forces killed. Hengaw (Norway-based, Apr 8): 6,620 military killed. IDF claims 6,000+ IRGC killed. Combined extrapolated estimate: ~9,100+. Iran's internet blackout severely limits independent verification; ACLED documented 3,000+ distinct conflict events across 29 of 31 provinces through mid-March. No Iranian deaths reported since ceasefire took effect Apr 7 (~192 hours). CNN Apr 14 satellite imagery shows Iran ACTIVELY RECONSTITUTING missile capabilities during the ceasefire; US intel: ~50% launchers survived first 40 days. OCHA Apr 3: 115,193 civilian units damaged, 763 schools, 334 health/emergency centers. WHO verified 23 attacks on healthcare in Iran → 9 deaths; 120+ heritage sites damaged. Iran executed at least 14 protesters Mar 17-Apr 6 (Hengaw). UNHCR $80M appeal for 2.8M affected. Source: Iran Health Ministry / Iran Red Crescent / HRANA / Iran International / Hengaw / IDF / OCHA / WHO / CNN / UNHCR / ACLED.

lebanese_killed.label: Lebanese Killed
lebanese_killed.note: 2,089-2,132+ killed (+43 past 24 hrs), 6,762+ wounded — Bint Jbeil NEAR-COMPLETE CAPTURE — Apr 14 Israel-Lebanon talks in Washington (first since 1983) — Qassem rejected as 'futile' — Hezbollah 30 rockets Apr 15
lebanese_killed.tooltip: Lebanese casualties from Israeli operations since March 2. Cumulative Apr 14-15: 2,089 killed (Public Health EOC Apr 14) / 2,124 (Health Ministry per TASS later update) / ~2,132 extrapolated Apr 15 with +43 past 24 hrs per manifest. 6,762+ wounded. 254 women + 168 children + 87-91 medical workers killed. UNICEF: 600+ children killed or injured since March 2. Apr 8 'Black Wednesday' single-day toll documented at 357 killed (50 jets, ~160 bombs, 100 targets in 10 minutes). IDF forced evacuation orders cover ~14% of Lebanon's territory (1,470 sq km). Displacement: 1-1.2M (~20% of population); 300,000+ fled to Syria (OCHA Apr 7); 140,000+ in 680 shelters (OCHA Apr 14). Battle of Bint Jbeil NEAR-COMPLETE per IDF — only 'several dozen' Hezbollah remain; 100+ killed since Apr 9 launch; IDF raided hospital sheltering armed Hezbollah. Apr 14 engagements: 1 IDF KIA + 13 total WIA; 10 paratroopers wounded (3 severely); 3 Hezbollah killed; 3 Radwan Force captured + Unit 504 interrogation; armored battalion commander seriously wounded. Apr 15: IDF 8th Brigade uncovered anti-tank launchers + weapons caches; struck Hezbollah command center. 5 IDF divisions operating. Hezbollah Apr 14: 24 attacks on northern Israel + IDF S. Lebanon positions; shot down Hermes 450 drone + fired SAMs at warplanes. Hezbollah Apr 15: ~30 rockets at northern Israel (IDF confirmed). 'Operation Silver Plough' demolishing border villages. Haaretz: IDF using 'methods similar to Gaza.' Lebanon-Israel direct talks Apr 14 (first since 1983 at State Dept) — Leiter + Moawad + Rubio — 2+ hours, Israel refused ceasefire commitment; Qassem rejected talks as 'futile,' 'free concession.' Lebanon Flash Appeal $308M — <1/3 funded. WHO: 133 healthcare attacks → 80+ killed. Source: Lebanon Public Health EOC / Lebanon MoH / TASS / IDF / UNICEF / NBC / WHO / UNHCR / Haaretz.

displaced.label: Displaced Persons
displaced.note: 4M+ regional displacement — Iran 3.2M+ IDPs (UNHCR Mar 12, likely higher) — Lebanon 1-1.2M (300K+ in Syria, 140K+ in 680 shelters) — 55,500 Afghan refugees returned — WHO Dubai hub ON HOLD
displaced.tooltip: People displaced from their homes across all fronts. Regional total: 4 million+. Iran: 3.2 million+ internally displaced (UNHCR Mar 12 — likely higher now), compounded by severe flooding in 24 provinces Mar 25-30 + additional flooding Apr 5 in Rudbar/Gilan. 55,500 Afghan refugees returned to Afghanistan from Iran. 4.4M Afghans in Iran particularly vulnerable. Lebanon internal: 1-1.2 million displaced (~20% of population, IOM/Lebanon disaster mgmt). Lebanon→Syria: 300,000+ fled (OCHA Apr 7). Lebanon in shelters: 140,000+ in 680 sites (OCHA Apr 14). Humanitarian access is collapsing. WHO's Dubai logistics hub — the global health emergency hub — remains ON HOLD due to regional insecurity, delaying 50+ emergency supply requests from 25 countries. Strait of Hormuz closure disrupted 80%+ of GCC food imports. UNHCR has issued an $80 million appeal for 2.8 million affected people in Iran. Lebanon Flash Appeal: $308M requested, less than one-third funded. Iran continued executing protesters during the war — at least 14 confirmed secret executions Mar 17-Apr 6 (Hengaw). Ceasefire on Iran front holds ~192 hours but Lebanon excluded — continued Israeli operations at Bint Jbeil. Source: UNHCR / WHO / OCHA / IOM / UNICEF / IFRC / Hengaw.

flights_cancelled.label: Flights Cancelled
flights_cancelled.note: 52,000+ flights — BLOCKADE DAY 3: 9 vessels turned back in 48 hrs per CENTCOM — Rich Starry + Elpis reversed course — 20+ non-Iran-bound ships passed freely — Iran threatens Red Sea counter-blockade
flights_cancelled.tooltip: Commercial flights cancelled due to airspace closures: 52,000+ since Feb 28. Hormuz blockade Day 3 (Apr 15, 48-hr CENTCOM update): 9 vessels total turned back, no breaches claimed. Adm. Brad Cooper: blockade 'fully implemented,' Iran sea trade 'completely halted.' WSJ: US forces intercepted 8 oil tankers entering/leaving Iranian ports. Reuters: US Navy destroyer interdicted 2 oil tankers from Chabahar via radio. Kpler: vessel Ostria turned back before loading at Iranian port. 20+ non-Iran-bound commercial ships passed through Strait freely (WSJ). Ambiguous: Rich Starry (Chinese-owned, sanctioned, false Malawian flag) exited Iran's outbound lane Apr 14 then REVERSED COURSE into Gulf of Oman; Elpis (sanctioned) stopped then turned off transponder; 5 laden product tankers exited Iran's corridor around blockade start, now holding stationary in Gulf of Oman. Iran's Tasnim claims commercial vessels 'set sail' despite blockade; CENTCOM denies breaches. CENTCOM's scope: Iranian ports only (vessels to non-Iranian ports permitted). UK, Australia decline to participate. France announces 'peaceful multinational mission' for Hormuz (separate from US); France/UK co-host Friday summit on multilateral freedom-of-navigation. China 'strongly condemned.' Qatar rejected 'any escalatory moves.' IRGC Maj. Gen. Abdollahi THREATENS counter-blockade of Persian Gulf + Sea of Oman + Red Sea. IEA April OMR: 10.1 mb/d March loss — largest in history; Hormuz 3.8 mb/d (80%+ decline). Blockade costs Iran ~$435M/day (FDD). Source: CENTCOM / WSJ / Reuters / Kpler / IEA / Tasnim / IATA / FDD.

children_killed.label: Children Killed
children_killed.note: Iran MoH: 212+ children — HRANA Iran: 15% of 3,636 — UNICEF Lebanon: 600+ killed or injured since March 2 — 168 Lebanese children killed — Pope Leo XIV feud with Trump intensifies
children_killed.tooltip: Children killed in the conflict. Iran MoH: 212+ children killed; HRANA: 15% of 3,636 total (~545). UNICEF reports 600+ children killed or injured in Lebanon since March 2; 168 Lebanese children killed total (up from 166). Apr 8 'Black Wednesday' toll: 357 killed including ~33 children. Key atrocities: Minab school attack (Feb 28, ~175 killed including schoolchildren) — HRW called it a war crime; Lamerd sports hall (4 children); Karaj bridge (8 civilians). 763 schools damaged in Iran, 65+ destroyed. 30+ universities struck. Pope Leo XIV feud with Trump intensifies: Pope called out 'delusion of omnipotence' fueling war; Trump attacked him on Truth Social; VP Vance told rally Pope should 'be careful' on 'matters of theology'; Cardinal McElroy: Iran war fails Catholic just war criteria. Pope currently on 11-day Africa trip (Algeria first). Iran executions during war include teen wrestler Saleh Mohammadi. Source: UNICEF / Iran MoH / HRANA / Lebanese MoH / WHO / HRW / Vatican / Hengaw.
